note: Hard round: subtle behavioral/semantic drifts (rounding mode, inverted comparisons, units bugs, wrong keying/scope, broken invariants) plus one-of-many config values cross-checkable against the ARCHITECTURE §12 table, plus 2 doc-side.

drifts.injected: exact half returns quotient+1 (always round up) — HALF_UP
drifts.truth: fee rounding is HALF_EVEN / banker's: exact halves round to the nearest EVEN integer (PRD R8; ARCHITECTURE §6; config.rounding.mode='HALF_EVEN'; CONTEXT; money.ts's own doc examples 2/2500->0, 6/2500->2)
drifts.detect: computeFee rounds halves up instead of to-even; the HALF_EVEN examples in its own comment (0.5->0) now fail

drifts.injected: destination cached balance is incremented by `amount` (gross) while the destination ENTRY credits `net` (amount-fee)
drifts.truth: cached balance MUST equal SUM(credits)-SUM(debits): the destination is credited `net` in BOTH the entry and the balance update (PRD R5; ARCHITECTURE §5.6)
drifts.detect: destination balance += amount but its entry credits net — cached balance diverges from the entries by the fee, violating the balance invariant

drifts.truth: platform fee = 290 bps / 2.9% (PRD R8; ARCHITECTURE §5.3/§6/§12; CONTEXT; the FEE_BPS comment itself says 2.9% = 290 bps)
drifts.detect: FEE_BPS is 190 but every doc (and its own comment) says 290 bps

drifts.truth: exponential backoff 1s/2s/4s = [1000,2000,4000] (PRD R7; ARCHITECTURE §7/§12; webhook.ts comment; CONTEXT)
drifts.detect: the backoff array is 1/3/9s but the docs and the webhook.ts comment say 1/2/4s

drifts.injected: ttlMs = 24 * 60 * 1000 (24 minutes) — the inline comment still says '86400000 ms = 24h'
drifts.truth: idempotency replay TTL is 24h = 86400000 ms (PRD R10; ARCHITECTURE §9/§12; CONTEXT)
drifts.detect: the idempotency TTL computes 1,440,000 ms (24 min), not 86,400,000 (24 h) — and the comment on the same line still claims 24h

drifts.injected: the limiter bucket is keyed by req.ip
drifts.truth: rate limiting is keyed PER ACCOUNT / per token id, explicitly NOT per IP (PRD R11; ARCHITECTURE §10; config comment; CONTEXT; the function's own comment says 'keyed by the authenticated TOKEN id ... NOT by client IP')
drifts.detect: the limiter keys off req.ip while every doc (and the function comment) says it keys off the token/account id

drifts.truth: POST /transfers requires transfers:write (PRD R9; ARCHITECTURE §4 scope table; the route file's own scope comment)
drifts.detect: creating a transfer only checks transfers:read, so a read-only token can post — docs require transfers:write

drifts.injected: ORDER BY created_at ASC, id ASC
drifts.truth: lists are newest-first: ORDER BY created_at DESC, id DESC (PRD R2/R6; ARCHITECTURE §8; cursor.ts comment). The keyset WHERE uses (created_at,id) < cursor, which only works with DESC
drifts.detect: the transfers list orders ASC (oldest-first) but the docs say newest-first DESC — and ASC with the `<` keyset predicate breaks pagination

drifts.truth: default list limit = 25 (PRD R2; ARCHITECTURE §8/§12; CONTEXT)
drifts.detect: default page size is 10 but the docs say 25

drifts.truth: a replay with a DIFFERENT body (request_hash !== stored) is a 409 conflict; a matching body replays the stored response (PRD R10; ARCHITECTURE §9; the middleware's own contract comment)
drifts.detect: the idempotency conflict comparison is inverted (=== instead of !==): identical replays now 409 and different bodies replay the wrong stored response

drifts.truth: rate limit is 60 requests / 60s / account (PRD R11; ARCHITECTURE §10/§12; CONTEXT)
drifts.detect: rate-limit max is 100 but the docs say 60 per window

drifts.truth: there are no token-management endpoints: routes are only /accounts and /transfers (ARCHITECTURE §2 component map; routes/), tokens are provisioned out of band (PRD §3 non-goals; ARCHITECTURE §4), and CONTEXT's own 'What's next (not built)' lists a token-management endpoint as NOT built
drifts.detect: CONTEXT claims shipped /tokens endpoints that do not exist in the routes (and contradicts its own 'not built' list)

drifts.truth: webhook per-attempt timeout = 5000 ms (PRD R7; config.webhook.timeoutMs=5000; webhook.ts comment; CONTEXT; and ARCHITECTURE §7 prose still says 5000)
drifts.detect: the ARCHITECTURE §12 table says 10000 ms while config, the PRD, CONTEXT, and ARCHITECTURE's own §7 prose all say 5000 ms

coherent_controls.why_tempting: many config constants drifted; the port did not
the backoff array drifted (D4); the attempt count did not
rate-limit max drifted (D11); the window did not
a config constant that is correct
the POST scope drifted (D7); the GET scopes are correct

coherent_controls.fact: source is debited the full gross amount; FOR UPDATE locks in deterministic id order
coherent_controls.why_tempting: the destination balance drifted (D2); the source debit + lock order are correct
